module.name: StaticContentSample
module.param: 'Discoverers' analyze the data you have indexed and suggest new search-time knowledge objects that will improve your experience in Splunk.

module.name: StaticContentSample
module.param:
• Discover fields

    (included in the app)

  • Discover reports

    (included in the app)

  • Discover event types

    (included in the app)

  • Discover transactions

    We've talked about these a lot but haven't built them -- this kind of tool could look at your data and suggest transactions based on either:

    1. finding single fields with high distinct count that look like ids. -- suggest simple transactions with | transaction foo
    2. finding non-overlapping sequences of events that look similar and that always seem to start the same or end the same -- suggest them as transactions that use the starts-with argument.
    3. finding sets of fields that look like they could be used to transitively link up more complex multi-field transactions.

  • Discover host tags

    One easy trick is to use the cluster command to break out your data by which sourcetypes are coming from which hosts. Sometimes this groups your hosts into the very same groups that you would want to assign tags to. (If it does or does not, let us know by clicking the links at the top right)

    eg: <any search here> | stats values(sourcetype) as sourcetypes by host | cluster field=sourcetypes labelonly=true | sort cluster_label | stats values(host) as hostValues values(sourcetypes) as sourcetypeValues by cluster_label
